sentences.sentence: According to a leading business consultancy, 3–14% of the global workforce will need to switch to a different occupation within the next 10–15 years, and all workers will need to adapt as their occupations evolve alongside increasingly capable machines.
sentences.translation: 据一家领先的商业咨询公司称，未来 10 到 15 年内，全球 3% 到 14% 的劳动力将需要转向不同职业，而所有劳动者都需要随着职业和越来越有能力的机器共同演变而适应变化。

structure.text: According to a leading business consultancy
will need to switch to a different occupation within the next 10–15 years
and all workers will need to adapt as their occupations evolve alongside increasingly capable machines
as their occupations evolve alongside increasingly capable machines

difficult_points: According to... 是来源状语，说明预测来自商业咨询机构。
3–14% of the global workforce 是百分比主语，核心含义是“一部分全球劳动力”。
and 连接两个并列预测：部分人换职业，所有人都要适应。
as 引导时间/伴随从句，表示职业会与更强大的机器一起变化。

sentences.sentence: Automation – or 'embodied artificial intelligence' (AI) – is one aspect of the disruptive effects of technology on the labour market.
sentences.translation: 自动化——或者说“具身人工智能”（AI）——是技术对劳动力市场产生颠覆性影响的一个方面。

structure.text: is one aspect of the disruptive effects of technology on the labour market
of technology on the labour market

difficult_points: 破折号中的 or... 是同位解释，把 Automation 解释为一种具身 AI。
one aspect of 表示“其中一个方面”，暗示技术影响不止自动化。
disruptive effects 指对既有劳动力市场格局的颠覆性影响。

sentences.sentence: 'Disembodied AI', like the algorithms running in our smartphones, is another.
sentences.translation: “非具身人工智能”，比如智能手机中运行的算法，则是另一个方面。

structure.text: like the algorithms running in our smartphones

difficult_points: like... 是插入例子，说明非具身 AI 的表现形式。
running in our smartphones 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 algorithms。
another 省略了 aspect，承接上一句的 one aspect。

sentences.sentence: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.
sentences.translation: 剑桥贾奇商学院的斯特拉·帕奇迪博士认为，一些最根本的变化正在发生，这是由于依赖数据而非生产的工作被“算法化”——也就是所谓知识经济中的工作。

structure.text: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes
that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production
as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs
that are dependent on data rather than on production

difficult_points: believes 后接 that 宾语从句，陈述 Pachidi 的核心观点。
as a result of 表示因果关系，指出变化来自工作算法化。
that are dependent on data rather than on production 修饰 jobs，界定知识经济中的岗位。
破折号后的 the so-called knowledge economy 对前面的 data-dependent jobs 作概括说明。

sentences.sentence: Algorithms are capable of learning from data to undertake tasks that previously needed human judgement, such as reading legal contracts, analysing medical scans and gathering market intelligence.
sentences.translation: 算法能够从数据中学习，从而承担过去需要人类判断的任务，例如阅读法律合同、分析医学扫描结果和收集市场情报。

structure.text: are capable of learning from data
to undertake tasks that previously needed human judgement
such as reading legal contracts, analysing medical scans and gathering market intelligence

difficult_points: are capable of 后接动名词 learning，表示能力。
to undertake tasks 表示算法学习数据后的目的或结果。
that previously needed human judgement 是定语从句，说明这些任务过去依赖人类判断。
such as 后列举三类知识型任务。

sentences.sentence: 'In many cases, they can outperform humans,' says Pachidi.
sentences.translation: 帕奇迪说：“在许多情况下，它们能够胜过人类。”
difficult_points: they 指代上一段中的 algorithms。
outperform 是比较含义动词，表示表现超过人类。
says Pachidi 是引语后的引述结构。

sentences.sentence: 'Organisations are attracted to using algorithms because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”, as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity.'
sentences.translation: “组织机构之所以被使用算法所吸引，是因为它们希望基于自己认为的‘完美信息’作出选择，同时降低成本并提高生产率。”

structure.text: because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”
as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity

difficult_points: are attracted to using algorithms 中 to 是介词，后接动名词 using。
because 引导原因从句，解释机构采用算法的动机。
what they consider is “perfect information” 是 based on 的宾语从句。
as well as 后接不定式，表示额外目的：降成本、提效率。

sentences.sentence: 'But these enhancements are not without consequences,' says Pachidi.
sentences.translation: 帕奇迪说：“但这些提升并非没有后果。”
difficult_points: But 引出对算法好处的转折，提示后文讨论问题。
not without consequences 是双重否定式表达，意思是“有后果”。
these enhancements 指代前文的降低成本、提升生产率等改善。

sentences.sentence: 'If routine cognitive tasks are taken over by AI, how do professions develop their future experts?' she asks.
sentences.translation: 她问道：“如果常规认知任务被 AI 接管，各行业该如何培养未来的专家呢？”

structure.text: 'If routine cognitive tasks are taken over by AI
how do professions develop their future experts

difficult_points: If 引导条件从句，假设常规认知任务被 AI 接管。
are taken over by 是被动语态，强调任务被 AI 承担。
how do professions develop... 是直接问句语序，关注专家培养问题。

sentences.sentence: 'One way of learning about a job is “legitimate peripheral participation” – a novice stands next to experts and learns by observation.
sentences.translation: “了解一份工作的一种方式是‘合法的边缘性参与’——新手站在专家身旁，通过观察来学习。

structure.text: 'One way of learning about a job
stands next to experts and learns by observation

difficult_points: One way of learning about a job 是动名词短语后置修饰 way，说明学习工作的方式。
破折号后用具体场景解释 “legitimate peripheral participation”。
by observation 表示学习方式，即通过观察而不是直接授课。

sentences.sentence: If this isn't happening, then you need to find new ways to learn.'
sentences.translation: 如果这种情况没有发生，那么你就需要找到新的学习方式。”

structure.text: need to find new ways to learn

difficult_points: this 指代上一句所说的新手观察专家的学习机制。
If... then... 构成条件结果关系。
new ways to learn 中 to learn 后置修饰 ways，说明方式的用途。

sentences.sentence: Another issue is the extent to which the technology influences or even controls the workforce.
sentences.translation: 另一个问题是，这项技术在多大程度上影响甚至控制劳动力。

structure.text: to which the technology influences or even controls the workforce
influences or even controls the workforce

difficult_points: the extent to which 是固定结构，表示“……的程度”。
influences or even controls 由轻到重递进，强调技术权力可能扩大。
the workforce 指整体劳动者群体。

sentences.sentence: For over two years, Pachidi monitored a telecommunications company.
sentences.translation: 在两年多的时间里，帕奇迪跟踪观察了一家电信公司。
difficult_points: For over two years 表示持续时间，强调研究观察时间较长。
monitored 在此表示持续跟踪观察，而不是简单“监视”。

sentences.sentence: 'The way telecoms salespeople work is through personal and frequent contact with clients, using the benefit of experience to assess a situation and reach a decision.
sentences.translation: “电信销售人员的工作方式是与客户保持个人化且频繁的接触，并利用经验优势来评估情况、作出决定。

structure.text: is through personal and frequent contact with clients
using the benefit of experience to assess a situation and reach a decision

difficult_points: The way...work 是主语结构，way 后省略关系副词 that/in which。
through personal and frequent contact 表示工作方式依赖与客户接触。
using... 是现在分词短语，说明销售人员如何判断并决策。

sentences.sentence: However, the company had started using an algorithm that defined when account managers should contact certain customers about which kinds of campaigns and what to offer them.'
sentences.translation: 然而，该公司已经开始使用一种算法，由它来规定客户经理应在何时就哪些活动联系特定客户，以及向他们提供什么。”

structure.text: that defined when account managers should contact certain customers about which kinds of campaigns and what to offer them
when account managers should contact certain customers

difficult_points: had started using 是过去完成时，表示算法使用早于叙述时点已经开始。
that defined... 是定语从句，说明算法的功能是规定销售行为。
when、which kinds 和 what 并列构成算法决定的内容。
what to offer them 是疑问词加不定式结构。

sentences.sentence: The algorithm – usually built by external designers – often becomes the keeper of knowledge, she explains.
sentences.translation: 她解释说，这种算法通常由外部设计者构建，往往会成为知识的掌管者。

structure.text: often becomes the keeper of knowledge

difficult_points: 破折号中的 usually built by external designers 是过去分词短语，补充算法来源。
becomes the keeper of knowledge 是比喻说法，表示知识和判断被算法掌控。
she explains 是插入式引述，说明这是 Pachidi 的解释。

sentences.sentence: In cases like this, Pachidi believes, a short-sighted view begins to creep into working practices whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions.
sentences.translation: 帕奇迪认为，在这类情况下，一种短视观点开始悄悄渗入工作实践：员工通过“算法的眼睛”学习，并逐渐依赖算法的指令。

structure.text: begins to creep into working practices
whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions

difficult_points: Pachidi believes 是插入语，主句主语是 a short-sighted view。
creep into 表示悄悄进入，强调这种观点逐步渗透工作实践。
whereby 引导关系从句，说明 working practices 中发生的学习方式。
become dependent on 表示员工对算法指令形成依赖。

sentences.sentence: Alternative explorations – where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas – are effectively discouraged.
sentences.translation: 其他探索方式——在这些探索中，实验和人类直觉会带来进步和新想法——实际上受到了抑制。

structure.text: where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas

difficult_points: 破折号中的 where 从句解释 alternative explorations 的特点。
lead to progress and new ideas 表示实验与直觉带来积极结果。
are effectively discouraged 是被动语态，表示这些探索实际上被压制。

sentences.sentence: Pachidi and colleagues even observed people developing strategies to make the algorithm work to their own advantage.
sentences.translation: 帕奇迪和同事甚至观察到，人们会制定策略，让算法为自己的利益服务。

structure.text: to make the algorithm work to their own advantage

difficult_points: observed people developing strategies 是“观察到某人正在做某事”的结构。
even 强调这种行为出乎意料或程度更进一步。
to make... 表示制定策略的目的。

sentences.sentence: 'We are seeing cases where workers feed the algorithm with false data to reach their targets,' she reports.
sentences.translation: 她报告说：“我们看到一些情况，员工为了达成目标，会向算法输入虚假数据。”

structure.text: where workers feed the algorithm with false data to reach their targets

difficult_points: are seeing 使用现在进行时，表示正在观察到这类案例。
where 引导定语从句，修饰 cases，说明案例中的具体行为。
feed the algorithm with false data 是把虚假数据提供给算法。
to reach their targets 表示这样做的目的。

expressions.text: feed the algorithm with false data

sentences.sentence: It's scenarios like these that many researchers are working to avoid.
sentences.translation: 许多研究人员正在努力避免的，正是这类情景。

structure.text: that many researchers are working to avoid

difficult_points: It's...that... 是强调结构，强调 scenarios like these。
scenarios like these 指代前文员工操纵算法等问题情景。
are working to avoid 表示正在努力避免，而不是已经避免。

sentences.sentence: Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made.
sentences.translation: 他们的目标是让 AI 技术更加可信和透明，以便组织和个人理解 AI 决策是如何作出的。

structure.text: is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent
to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent
so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made

difficult_points: Their 指代 many researchers。
to make... 是表语不定式，说明 objective 的内容。
so that 引导目的从句，说明提高透明度的目的。
how AI decisions are made 是 understand 的宾语从句，含被动语态。

sentences.sentence: In the meantime, says Pachidi, 'We need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control.'
sentences.translation: 帕奇迪说：“与此同时，我们需要确保自己充分理解这个新世界在专业知识、职业边界和控制权方面提出的困境。”

structure.text: need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas
that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control

difficult_points: In the meantime 表示在研究者解决问题之前的过渡阶段。
make sure 后省略 that，引出需要确保理解的内容。
that...raises 是定语从句，修饰 dilemmas。
regarding 后列出困境涉及的三个方面。

sentences.sentence: Economist Professor Hamish Low believes that the future of work will involve major transitions across the whole life course for everyone: 'The traditional trajectory of full-time education followed by full-time work followed by a pensioned retirement is a thing of the past,' says Low.
sentences.translation: 经济学家哈米什·洛教授认为，工作的未来将涉及每个人整个生命历程中的重大转变；洛说：“全日制教育之后是全职工作，再之后是领取养老金的退休，这种传统轨迹已经成为过去。”

structure.text: that the future of work will involve major transitions across the whole life course for everyone
'The traditional trajectory of full-time education followed by full-time work followed by a pensioned retirement
is a thing of the past

difficult_points: believes 后的 that 从句概括 Low 对未来工作的看法。
across the whole life course 表示这种转变贯穿整个生命历程。
followed by... followed by... 两个过去分词短语后置修饰 trajectory。
a thing of the past 表示传统模式已经过时。

sentences.sentence: Instead, he envisages a multistage employment life: one where retraining happens across the life course, and where multiple jobs and no job happen by choice at different stages.
sentences.translation: 相反，他设想的是一种多阶段的就业人生：在这种人生中，再培训贯穿整个生命历程，并且在不同阶段，人们会出于选择从事多份工作或不工作。

structure.text: one where retraining happens across the life course, and where multiple jobs and no job happen by choice at different stages
where retraining happens across the life course

difficult_points: Instead 表明 Low 提出的是替代传统三阶段人生的模式。
one 指代 a multistage employment life，冒号后作进一步解释。
两个 where 从句并列，分别说明再培训和就业状态的变化。
by choice 表示这些变化来自个人选择，而不是被迫。

sentences.sentence: On the subject of job losses, Low believes the predictions are founded on a fallacy: 'It assumes that the number of jobs is fixed.
sentences.translation: 关于岗位流失的问题，洛认为这些预测建立在一个谬误之上：“它假定工作岗位的数量是固定的。

structure.text: On the subject of job losses
the predictions are founded on a fallacy
that the number of jobs is fixed

difficult_points: On the subject of... 表示话题转换到岗位流失。
believes 后省略 that，引导宾语从句。
are founded on a fallacy 是被动结构，表示预测建立在错误前提上。
the number of jobs is fixed 是 assumes 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: If in 30 years, half of 100 jobs are being carried out by robots, that doesn't mean we are left with just 50 jobs for humans.
sentences.translation: 如果 30 年后，100 个岗位中有一半由机器人完成，那并不意味着人类只剩下 50 个岗位。

structure.text: If in 30 years, half of 100 jobs are being carried out by robots
are being carried out by robots
we are left with just 50 jobs for humans

difficult_points: If 引导假设条件，从句中的 in 30 years 是时间状语。
are being carried out 是进行时被动语态，表示岗位任务由机器人执行。
that 指代前面整个假设。
are left with 表示“剩下、留下”。

sentences.sentence: The number of jobs will increase: we would expect there to be 150 jobs.'
sentences.translation: 岗位数量会增加：我们预计会有 150 个岗位。”
difficult_points: The number of jobs 作主语，谓语用单数概念的 will increase。
冒号后解释岗位数增加的具体预期。
expect there to be 是 expect 后接 there be 非谓语结构。

sentences.sentence: Dr Ewan McGaughey, at Cambridge's Centre for Business Research and King's College London, agrees that 'apocalyptic' views about the future of work are misguided.
sentences.translation: 剑桥商业研究中心和伦敦国王学院的伊万·麦高伊博士同意，关于工作未来的“末日论”观点是错误的。

structure.text: at Cambridge's Centre for Business Research and King's College London
that 'apocalyptic' views about the future of work are misguided

difficult_points: 两个逗号之间的 at... 是插入信息，说明 McGaughey 的机构。
agrees 后接 that 宾语从句，表达他同意的观点。
'apocalyptic' views 使用引号，表示类似“末日式”的夸张看法。
misguided 表示判断错误或被误导。

sentences.sentence: 'It's the laws that restrict the supply of capital to the job market, not the advent of new technologies that causes unemployment.'
sentences.translation: “造成失业的是限制资本供给进入就业市场的法律，而不是新技术的出现。”

structure.text: that restrict the supply of capital to the job market
not the advent of new technologies

difficult_points: It's...that... 是强调结构，强调 laws 才是原因。
not the advent of new technologies 与 the laws 构成对比。
the supply of capital to the job market 指进入就业市场的资本供给。
causes unemployment 的逻辑主语是被强调的因素，而非 technologies 本身。

sentences.sentence: His recently published research answers the question of whether automation, AI and robotics will mean a 'jobless future' by looking at the causes of unemployment.
sentences.translation: 他最近发表的研究通过考察失业的原因，回答了自动化、AI 和机器人技术是否会意味着“无工作未来”这一问题。

structure.text: of whether automation, AI and robotics will mean a 'jobless future'
by looking at the causes of unemployment

difficult_points: recently published 是过去分词短语，修饰 research。
the question of whether... 表示“是否……的问题”。
by looking at... 说明研究回答问题的方法。
'jobless future' 用引号突出这一争议性说法。

sentences.sentence: 'History is clear that change can mean redundancies.
sentences.translation: “历史清楚地表明，变化可能意味着裁员。
difficult_points: History is clear that... 用历史作主语，表示历史证据很明确。
that 从句说明 clear 的具体内容。
redundancies 在就业语境中指岗位冗余或裁员。

sentences.sentence: But social policies can tackle this through retraining and redeployment.'
sentences.translation: 但社会政策可以通过再培训和重新部署来应对这一问题。”
difficult_points: But 表示虽然变化会带来裁员，但政策可以应对。
this 指代上一句提到的 redundancies。
through retraining and redeployment 表示解决问题的手段。

sentences.sentence: He adds: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security.
sentences.translation: 他补充说：“如果 AI 和机器人技术将会导致工作发生变化，那么我希望看到各国政府抓住机会改进政策，以保障良好的就业安全。

structure.text: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics
then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity

difficult_points: If...then... 构成条件结果关系，讨论 AI 和机器人带来的变化。
there is going to be change to jobs 表示工作将发生变化。
I'd like to see governments seizing... 是 see someone doing 的结构。
to improve policy 和 to enforce... 表示政府抓住机会的目的。

sentences.sentence: We can “reprogramme” the law to prepare for a fairer future of work and leisure.'
sentences.translation: 我们可以‘重新编程’法律，为一个更公平的工作与休闲未来做准备。”

structure.text: to prepare for a fairer future of work and leisure
a fairer future of work and leisure

difficult_points: “reprogramme” 用引号表明是比喻，把修改法律比作重新编程。
to prepare for... 表示重新设计法律的目的。
work and leisure 并列，说明未来不仅涉及就业，也涉及闲暇。

sentences.sentence: McGaughey's findings are a call to arms to leaders of organisations, governments and banks to pre-empt the coming changes with bold new policies that guarantee full employment, fair incomes and a thriving economic democracy.
sentences.translation: 麦高伊的发现是对组织、政府和银行领导者的行动号召，呼吁他们用大胆的新政策预先应对即将到来的变化，这些政策要保障充分就业、公平收入和蓬勃发展的经济民主。

structure.text: are a call to arms to leaders of organisations, governments and banks
to pre-empt the coming changes with bold new policies
that guarantee full employment, fair incomes and a thriving economic democracy

difficult_points: a call to arms 是比喻表达，表示强烈行动号召。
to pre-empt... 后置修饰 call to arms，说明被号召者应采取的行动。
that guarantee... 是定语从句，修饰 policies。
full employment, fair incomes and a thriving economic democracy 是并列保障目标。

expressions.meaning_zh: 预先应对即将到来的变化

sentences.sentence: 'The promises of these new technologies are astounding.
sentences.translation: “这些新技术的前景令人震惊。
difficult_points: promises 在这里指技术带来的潜力或前景，而不是具体承诺。
astounding 表示非常惊人，强调新技术的潜在影响巨大。

sentences.sentence: They deliver humankind the capacity to live in a way that nobody could have once imagined,' he adds.
sentences.translation: 它们赋予人类一种能力，使人类能够以过去无人想象过的方式生活，”他补充道。

structure.text: to live in a way that nobody could have once imagined
that nobody could have once imagined

difficult_points: They 指代上一句的 new technologies。
deliver humankind the capacity 是双宾语结构，表示赋予人类能力。
that nobody could have once imagined 是定语从句，修饰 way。
could have once imagined 表示过去本可能想象到但实际上难以想象。

sentences.sentence: 'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.
sentences.translation: “正如工业革命让人们越过自给农业阶段，企业革命使大规模生产成为可能，第三次革命也已经被宣告到来。

structure.text: 'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production

difficult_points: Just as 引导类比结构，把第三次革命与工业革命、企业革命相比较。
brought people past subsistence agriculture 表示使人类超越自给农业阶段。
enabled mass production 表示企业革命使大规模生产成为可能。
has been pronounced 是现在完成时被动语态，表示第三次革命已被宣告。

sentences.sentence: But it will not only be one of technology.
sentences.translation: 但它不会只是一次技术革命。

structure.text: will not only be one of technology

difficult_points: it 指代上一句的 a third revolution。
not only 表示它不只是技术层面的革命，为下一句 social 作铺垫。
one of technology 中 one 指 revolution。

sentences.sentence: The next revolution will be social.'
sentences.translation: 下一次革命将是社会性的。”
difficult_points: The next revolution 指上一句所说第三次革命之后的社会层面转变。
social 作表语，强调这场革命的核心不仅是技术，而是社会制度和生活方式。
